mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in FEDFUNDS transmits through lower discount rates and easier financial conditions, lifting equity valuations and supporting positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Reject if FEDFUNDS declines but 20-day SPX forward returns are negative in more than 60% of easing instances, or if rolling 2-year Rank IC stays below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL inflation accelerates enough to force FEDFUNDS higher, real discount rates and recession risk rise, which should produce negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL rises above its 6-month trend and FEDFUNDS is at or above 4.5%
failure_condition: Reject if the triggered inflation-plus-tightening state is followed by positive 20-day SPX returns in more than 60% of cases, or if the sign of the effect is not negative in out-of-sample rolling tests.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in UNRATE from the prior month signals weakening household income and earnings expectations, which should pressure equities lower over the next month.
failure_condition: Reject if month-over-month increases in UNRATE do not precede negative 20-day SPX returns more often than chance, or if rolling 3-year Rank IC is below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous increase in PAYEMS and RSAFS indicates improving payroll income and retail demand, supporting near-term earnings expectations and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS and RSAFS both rise versus their prior releases
failure_condition: Reject if the joint rise in PAYEMS and RSAFS is followed by negative 5-day SPX returns in more than 55% of triggered instances, or if the interaction term adds no predictive value versus single-series models.

mechanism_narrative: A threshold breach in INDPRO above its recent baseline signals factory output acceleration and stronger cyclical growth, which should generate positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO rises by more than 0.5% over 2 months
failure_condition: Reject if INDPRO threshold breakouts fail to precede positive 20-day SPX returns more than 50% of the time, or if the signal's Rank IC is indistinguishable from zero over rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in HOUST from its recent level signals housing weakness and a coming slowdown in cyclical demand, which should lead to negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls by more than 8% from its 3-month average
failure_condition: Reject if housing downside breaks are followed by positive 60-day SPX returns in more than 55% of cases, or if the signal loses sign stability in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: GDP growth and consumer spending growth must both weaken to signal a broad demand slowdown; when GDPC1 and RSAFS decelerate together, earnings revisions should turn lower and SPX returns over the next quarter should be negative.
failure_condition: Reject if joint deceleration in GDPC1 and RSAFS does not predict negative 60-day SPX returns better than either series alone, or if the combined signal has Rank IC below 0.03 over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-shock recovery, a falling UNRATE combined with rising PAYEMS confirms labor-market normalization, which supports risk appetite and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if simultaneous declines in UNRATE and gains in PAYEMS are followed by negative 20-day SPX returns in more than 60% of instances, or if the interaction effect disappears after controlling for either series alone.
